title: Sunny Queen 12 Extra Large Free Range Eggs 700g $7.00 (Was $8.80) @ Woolworths
body:
Deal: Sunny Queen 12 Extra Large Free Range Eggs 700g $7.00 (Was $8.80) @ Woolworths

[hemisphere] It might not be for you but some people I know they are very particular about the products and the brands they buy. So, when there are sales, they will buy them quickly.
[Brisbane1] I miss the days when people posted actual deals on OzBargain
[hemisphere] This might not sound like a good deal for you but it might be for some other people who are particular about what they buy. Saving $1.80 does not seem a lot but put that saving together with other things, this will add up.
[N B] At ALDI 700g free range is $6.19 . Which one is better?
[hemisphere] Sorry, I dont know which is better as I am cheap and only buy the cheapest eggs per weight. The cheapest eggs now is Cage Free Eggs 12 Pack @Coles which cost $0.80/100G.
Check it here: https://cartsavvy.com.au/?search=eggs&category=Dairy+%26+Egg…
[sherbules] We buy homebrand of most stuff where it is cheaper and quality seems similar, but we do buy sunny queen eggs as it’s 1500 hens per hectare compared to other free range eggs which are 10000 hens per hectare
So it does cost more than caged/barn/other free range but the hens have heaps more space. Cant say there is a taste difference though, not an egg connoisseur haha
[maverickjohn] That one's is more for the chickens quality of life. Taste has little impact.
I buy the 1500 hens /hectare just because I think it's a nicer thing to do. But each to their own.
When I get the Parklea free ones I don't really consider the source. But where possible I'll try.
[Lizard Spock] How exactly are you associated with Woolworths and Coles? Also, 2 of your previous Coles and Woolworths posts (from last year) you did not indicate you were associated.
[hemisphere] I dont associate with Colesworths but I built a grocery price comparison website called CartSavvy .
[Lizard Spock] I see. So more of a third party, rather than associated.
[M00Cow] local veggos have 2 x 600g for $7.00 or 700g $5
[Chocobros] This is the real deal. Also supporting the local veggos over colesworth is a double win
[hemisphere] Yeah, my local Asian store has $8 of 700g for 2 pax of the egg. This deal might not be for everyone as free range is more expensive than the regular eggs.
[rhino86] Go to your local green grocer and find eggs cheaper.
Sometimes they have 2x dozen for $5. Meggles farm large eggs.
Dinmore and Booval QLD.
[carrawa] Might depend on your individual store. Check out your local Silly Solly's. They had 600g eggs initially for about $3.00 dozen, last I saw a few weeks ago $3.50/$4.00.
[maverickjohn] Parklea markets give you free carton of eggs for every 2kg of meat you buy.
It's good that their meat prices are decent as well
[ohgee] Where did you bring them from? And even more curious to where did you take them?
[maxwin] Huh every non major corp fruit market sells eggs at 3.99 a dozen or 3 for 7.50 or 3 bucks each. What people rely on colesworth for everything?
[liam2040] be careful, my vegan satire didn't land sunny side up
[Rrpnothanx] These say 1500 hens per hectare.
Should be happier hens compared with the 10000 hens per hectare.
Hope the yolk is nicer in colour and more tasty. Will give it a go.
[WwDesi] I can buy fresh from my local market $15 for 30 eggs
[Bimbobob] Pretty regular pricing lately, seems overstock tbh as at least with my two local stores they seem to alternate the discounts
[BlitzR] After watching this https://youtu.be/JjSOawvt-6o?si=71JAdetEZKZGrlx8 you'll see that majority of so-called free range eggs are a scam
